findings.message: New data-layer write path `delete_items_by_prefix` performs a select-then-bulk-delete against the datastore with no span or metric instrumentation. The delete (and its row count / latency) is invisible to observability — operators cannot see how often it runs, how long it takes, or how many rows it removes.
findings.suggestion: Wrap the query/delete in a tracing span and emit metrics: a latency histogram and a deleted-row-count counter (e.g. via OpenTelemetry/Prometheus), so the bulk-delete path is observable. Log datastore errors with the request correlation id.

findings.message: The `select(Item).where(Item.name.startswith(prefix))` query is unbounded (no limit/pagination) and materialized into a list, yet has no latency or row-count metric around it. A large prefix match can load and delete an unbounded number of rows with no observability signal.
findings.suggestion: Add a row-count/latency metric around the query and consider batching/limiting the delete, mirroring the bounded read convention already used in `list_items` (MAX_PAGE_SIZE).
